title: Generate for loop makes unsigned var leading to false INFINITELOOP
body:
Hello:

I'm using verilator-4.106, and here is my testcase:
```verilog
// demo.v
module t #(
    parameter   PIPE = 4
)(
    input               clk,
    input   [15:0]      rem_i,
    output  [1:0]       rem_o[PIPE]
);

genvar i;
generate
for (i = 0; i < PIPE; i++) begin: SUB_PIPE

    localparam P_START = i + 1;
    localparam P_STOP  = i;

    sub #(
        .P_STOP     (P_STOP)
    ) U_SUB (
        .clk        (clk),
        .rem_i      (rem_i),
        .rem_o      (rem_o[PIPE])
    );

end
endgenerate

endmodule

//
module sub #(
    parameter P_START = 1,
    parameter P_STOP  = 0
)(
    input               clk,
    input       [15:0]  rem_i,
    output  reg [1:0]   rem_o
);

always @ (*) begin
    for (int i = P_START; i >= P_STOP; i--) begin
        rem_o[i] = rem_i[i+1];
    end
end

endmodule

```
```
verilator --cc demo.v
```
```
%Warning-UNSIGNED: demo.v:41:29: Comparison is constant due to unsigned arithmetic
                               : ... In instance t.SUB_PIPE[0].U_SUB
   41 |     for (int i = P_START; i >= P_STOP; i--) begin
      |                             ^~
                   ... Use "/* verilator lint_off UNSIGNED */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
%Warning-INFINITELOOP: demo.v:41:5: Infinite loop (condition always true)
                                  : ... In instance t.SUB_PIPE[0].U_SUB
   41 |     for (int i = P_START; i >= P_STOP; i--) begin
      |     ^~~
- V3Simulate.h:348:   Unknown node type in SimulateVisitor: WHILE
%Error: Exiting due to 2 warning(s)
```

I can't find any "Infinite loop" in my testcase, but veriltor report a Warning-INFINITELOOP.
In my opinion, it is a incorrect report.
What do you think? Is this warning reported correctly?

If you agree with me, I think the reason is that:
Verilator mark the local parameter "P_STOP" in module "t" as a unsigned value of 0,1,2,3 when unrolling the genfor statements in `V3Unroll.cpp`, and then the property would be propagated to the for statement in module "sub", making the condition of comparing with a unsigned value always true.

I don't know why the value always be unsigned as unrolling. Maybe keep it signed can just fix this problem.
